meta_title: For Students: analytics setup to Solve no testing knowledge (Intermediate Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to analytics setup and overcome no testing knowledge. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Analytics Setup for Students: Solve No Testing Knowledge
content.seo_description: Bridge the gap between code and user behavior. A complete guide for students to implement robust analytics using Antigravity and Claude Code.
content.answer_block: You are flying blind without analytics. By integrating event-based tracking, you transform raw usage data into a feedback loop that replaces formal QA. Use Antigravity to instrument your core user flows and Claude Code to automate the implementation, turning your app into a self-validating, data-driven engine.
content.intro:
You’ve pushed your first commit, the UI looks sharp, and the logic holds up in your local environment. But then the silence hits. Without a testing suite, you have no idea if your app is actually solving user problems or silently crashing in the wild. You are building in a vacuum, and that is where most student projects go to die.

It is time to stop guessing and start observing. By instrumenting your application with high-fidelity analytics, you gain a diagnostic lens that reveals exactly where users struggle. This isn't just about tracking clicks; it is about building a professional-grade telemetry system that turns every user interaction into a actionable performance metric.

steps.title: Define Your North Star Metrics
steps.description: Before writing code, identify the three events that signify a successful user journey. Avoid tracking everything; focus on the 'aha!' moments.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my current SaaS codebase and suggest the top 5 critical user interaction points that should be tracked to measure core feature adoption.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'identify key user journey events in src/components'

steps.title: Initialize Analytics Provider
steps.description: Standardize your tracking interface so you can swap providers later. Create a unified analytics utility file.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a robust, type-safe analytics wrapper utility that supports event logging and user property tracking.

steps.title: Instrument Critical Flows
steps.description: Inject tracking calls into your signup, primary CTA, and error handling blocks to capture real-world usage patterns.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor my sign-up flow to include non-blocking analytics events for success and failure states.

steps.title: Automate Error Telemetry
steps.description: Use analytics to catch what unit tests miss by logging unhandled promise rejections and UI crashes.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a global error boundary component that automatically sends error stack traces to my analytics dashboard.

steps.title: Build a Dashboard View
steps.description: Visualize your data. If you can't see the trend, you can't improve the product.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a script to fetch my last 24 hours of event data and aggregate it into a simple CLI summary table.

pro_tips: Track user 'rage clicks'—multiple rapid clicks in one area often signal a broken UI state that tests missed.
Always sanitize PII (Personally Identifiable Information) before sending data to your analytics provider to maintain user trust.
Use event metadata to capture the specific version of your app; this allows you to compare performance before and after updates.
Implement 'sampling' if your traffic grows to keep costs low while maintaining statistically significant data.
Don't just track events; track the time taken between events to measure friction in your user onboarding.
content.next_steps: Once your telemetry is live, spend one week observing the heatmaps, identify the highest drop-off rate, and use Claude Code to run an A/B test on that specific component.
content.conversion_block: Stop guessing if your code works and start proving it with data. Your transition from a student coder to a professional engineer starts with visibility. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
